message:
feat(rch): improve status display with elapsed time and smart truncation

Enhances the rch status output in two ways:

1. Elapsed time display:
   - Active jobs now show elapsed time (e.g., "2m 30s") instead of raw
     ISO 8601 timestamps
   - Added elapsed_since() to parse timestamps and calculate duration
   - Added format_elapsed() for human-friendly time formatting
   - Works in both table and plain-text output modes

2. Intelligent command truncation:
   - truncate_command() now preserves important suffixes like --release,
     --test, --lib, --bin, --workspace when truncating long commands
   - Attempts to preserve -p <package> patterns for visibility
   - Falls back to simple truncation only when smart preservation isn't
     possible

This makes "rch status" much more useful for at-a-glance monitoring:
- See how long jobs have been running, not when they started
- Quickly identify release vs debug builds even in truncated output

Includes tests for truncation edge cases and elapsed time formatting.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
